evals.prompt: I have a 7-step runbook for onboarding new studio vendors. Help me turn it into a skill my agent can run from a brief URL.
evals.expected_output: Rich skill folder plan or draft with Purpose, Workflow steps marked deterministic/agentic, validation checklist from runbook, references/adapters.md for tool bindings, and eval cases. Points to skill-creation-best-practices.md rather than duplicating it.

evals.prompt: We just finished a workflow that searched Jira, pulled account IDs, and opened a CR. Save this as a reusable skill called endpoint-onboarding.
evals.expected_output: Session extraction workflow: gather trigger/inputs, skeleton with session tools only, structured steps with Validate and On failure, Lessons Learned section, offer to save and generate evals.

evals.prompt: My csv-analysis skill never triggers when users say 'look at this spreadsheet'. The body is fine. Fix the description.
evals.expected_output: Revised imperative description naming spreadsheet/CSV/tabular intent and when to activate. May reference trigger eval methodology. Does not rewrite unrelated workflow sections.

evals.prompt: Author a skill that uses save_skill and extract_session_data. I'm on Cursor only.
evals.expected_output: Core workflow with function names; Quick tools mapped in references/adapters.md with Cursor omit paths (manual skeleton, Write tool save). Does not pretend Quick tools exist on Cursor.

assertions_notes: Add concrete assertions after first runs. Examples: 'references/adapters.md exists', 'description under 1024 chars', 'no banned words', 'steps have On failure'.
